commit.message:
Copilot/update code using design docs (#4)

* Initial plan

* Refactor to new layered architecture

Introduce a layered module structure under lib/src/:

- error/: typed exception hierarchy (FlacMetadataException,
  InvalidFlacException, MalformedMetadataException, etc.) with
  FlacCorruptHeaderException as a backward-compatible alias

- model/: immutable domain model — FlacMetadataBlock abstract base,
  concrete block types (StreamInfoBlock, VorbisCommentBlock, PictureBlock,
  PaddingBlock, ApplicationBlock, SeekTableBlock, CueSheetBlock,
  UnknownBlock), value types (VorbisComments/VorbisCommentEntry,
  PictureType enum, FlacBlockType enum), FlacMetadataDocument

- binary/: byte-level I/O — ByteReader, ByteWriter, FlacParser
  (returns FlacMetadataDocument), FlacSerializer

- edit/: mutation API — sealed MetadataMutation hierarchy,
  FlacMetadataEditor, PaddingStrategy

- transform/: FlacTransformer, FlacTransformPlan/Result/Options

- api/: top-level convenience functions (readFlacMetadata,
  applyMutations, transformFlac)

Remove old flat files (models.dart, flac_parser.dart, flac_editor.dart,
vorbis_comment_processor.dart, picture_block_processor.dart).

Update lib/dart_metaflac.dart to export all new symbols.
Update bin/metaflac.dart and all 4 test files to use the new API.

All 25 tests pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
khonliang-bus v0.1: cross-app event bus and service registry (#1)

* Initial khonliang-bus v0.1: cross-app event bus

Go service with HTTP + WebSocket transport, memory and SQLite backends,
service registry, and Go + Python clients. Python client auto-registers
on init.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Address Copilot review on khonliang-bus#1

Critical fixes:
- Fix lock-order deadlock in memory and sqlite backends. Publish now
  snapshots subscribers under the backend lock and delivers outside it,
  so it never holds backend lock while taking subscription locks.
  Close() acquires backend lock first to honor the order.
- Fix backfill ordering: subscriptions are now marked "live" only after
  backfill completes, with a drain-loop that catches new messages
  arriving during backfill before flipping atomically.

Security:
- Remove WebSocket InsecureSkipVerify; use OriginPatterns from config
  (defaults to same-host enforcement).
- Add Server WithAllowedOrigins option and config field.

Polish:
- Use StatusNormalClosure as the default WebSocket close code, with
  specific codes for protocol/policy/internal errors.
- Update storage.Backend docstring with accurate fromID semantics and
  the in-order delivery contract.
- Fix Python test fixture: drop redundant Popen, handle TimeoutExpired
  in teardown with kill() fallback.

New tests:
- TestConcurrentPublishAndClose stresses publish/close interleaving
  to catch deadlock regressions.
- TestBackfillBeforeLive verifies in-order delivery across the
  backfill→live transition.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Address second Copilot review pass on khonliang-bus#1

Correctness:
- Subscribe with empty fromID and no prior ack now anchors to the
  current tail (only new messages), matching the documented contract.
  Previously a fresh subscriber received the entire topic history.
- Ack no longer regresses last_acked_id on out-of-order acks. Memory
  uses idGreater + max(); sqlite uses a CASE expression in the upsert.

Robustness:
- idLess/idGreater now validate parses via parseMessageID and return
  false on invalid input instead of silently coercing to 0.
- sqlite Subscribe rejects non-numeric fromID up front.
- sqlite drainOnce returns explicit errors and checks rows.Err(); the
  backfill loop closes the subscription on unrecoverable errors so
  consumers don't block on Messages() forever.

Resource cleanup:
- Go client Subscription.readLoop closes the underlying WebSocket on
  exit so callers don't leak connections.

Retention wiring:
- Added Backend.Topics() and a retentionLoop that periodically iterates
  topics and calls Trim() with the configured messages_ttl. Previously
  the config field was unused.

Tests:
- Replaced fixed sleeps in Go and Python end-to-end tests with
  publish-and-poll loops bounded by an overall deadline.
- Added TestSubscribeWithoutAckStartsAtTail and TestAckDoesNotRegress
  for both backends, plus TestTopics for sqlite.
- Updated TestBackfillBeforeLive to opt into full replay via fromID="0".
- Fixed Python skip message to reference the actual go build command.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Address third Copilot review pass on khonliang-bus#1

- main: ensure sqlite parent dir exists before opening the DB so a
  fresh install doesn't fail with "unable to open database file".
- memory: validate fromID up front for parity with sqlite. Invalid
  IDs now return a clear error from Subscribe instead of being
  silently coerced.
- Go client: parse incoming WebSocket frames as a typed envelope.
  Server-side {"type":"error",...} frames now surface via a new
  Subscription.Err() accessor and terminate the stream instead of
  being delivered as fake zero-value Messages.
- Python test fixture: capture stdout/stderr and fail loudly with
  diagnostics on startup error instead of skipping silently.
- Python test: await the consumer task after cancel() so we don't
  leak pending tasks or trigger "Task was destroyed" warnings.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Address fourth Copilot review pass on khonliang-bus#1

keeps FTL running as root and bypasses the capability elevation.

CRITICAL FIX: Use API_KEY not HF_TOKEN, use os.environ not os.getenv

The validator explicitly states in error message:
'In this validator, the injected key is API_KEY, not HF_TOKEN'

Key changes:
1. Changed HF_TOKEN -> API_KEY
2. Use os.environ[...] directly (not os.getenv with defaults)
3. This ensures we use EXACTLY what validator provides

Why 11+ attempts failed:
- Documentation said HF_TOKEN, but validator provides API_KEY
- We were reading the WRONG variable name
- This is the actual root cause

New format (as validator requires):
  API_BASE_URL = os.environ['API_BASE_URL']
  API_KEY = os.environ['API_KEY']
  MODEL_NAME = os.environ.get('MODEL_NAME', default)

  client = OpenAI(
      base_url=API_BASE_URL,
      api_key=API_KEY
  )

commit.message:
fix: wrap OTEL_TRACES_SAMPLER_ARG in single quotes for Flux substitution

Flux's varSubstitution() does a YAML round-trip that strips double quotes.
After envsubst, bare 0.01 is parsed as a YAML float → K8s rejects it.
Wrapping in single quotes ('0.01') forces YAML single-quoted scalar = always
a string. Known Flux issue: fluxcd/flux2#2957.

commit.message:
Wire MAP_SELF hypercall through the full stack (Phase B step 2)

Add THEMIS_MAP_SELF (opcode 0x1f) across the standard 4-file hypercall
pattern:

  themis-abi:    THEMIS_MAP_SELF = 0x1f opcode constant
  hypercall.rs:  dispatch arm + do_map_self handler
                 (shared lock — map_self is non-destructive)
  thhv.h:        THEMIS_HC/OP_MAP_SELF defines + declaration
  thhv_hvcall.c: themis_map_self(cap_handle, new_gpa) wrapper

Register convention: RDI = cap_handle, RSI = new_gpa, RAX = error.
The handler calls Capability::map_self() via execute() with shared
lock. apply_update already handles ChangeRights updates from the
refcounted AddressMap diff — no platform.rs changes needed.

commit.message:
Fix Cloudflare timeout with fire-and-poll pattern

All POST endpoints now return immediately with a task_id. The handler
runs in a background thread and stores its result in a server-side
_tasks dict. The frontend polls GET /api/task/{id} every 3s until the
task completes.

Also fixes api() JS function to check resp.ok before parsing JSON,
preventing the 'Unexpected token <' error on HTML error pages.

commit.message:
refactor: apply DoD fixes across codebase

- Replace Obstacle.scored bool with ScoredTag existential component
  (eliminates if-checks in collision, burnout, and scoring systems)
- Remove BurnoutState.has_threat redundancy (use nearest_threat == entt::null)
- Move AudioQueue.push/clear and InputState.clear_events to free functions
- Extract input handling from main.cpp into input_system.cpp
- Move GamePhase::Playing guard into each system (systems self-guard)
- Remove dead ParticleEmitter component (unused by any system)
- Remove dead open_lane variable in obstacle_spawn_system
- Scoring system now removes Obstacle+ScoredTag after processing
  (eliminates base_points=0 sentinel in render_system)
- Add quit_requested field to InputState (replaces bool running in main)
- Clean up main.cpp: flat system pipeline, no phase branching, minimal includes
